diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous mucosal (wet) bleeding indicates a critically low platelet count.
Active mucosal bleeding is the highest-risk bleeding phenotype and the marker of emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse non-blanching petechiae on dependent lower limbs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Petechiae are the cutaneous signature of thrombocytopenia.
A non-blanching petechial rash in a child mandates active exclusion of meningococcaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Spontaneous large painless ecchymoses without trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous bruising without trauma reflects impaired primary haemostasis from low platelets.
A true petechial rash with mucosal bleeding points to a systemic haematological defect rather than localised inflicted trauma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent self-limiting viral upper respiratory illness ~2 weeks prior

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A preceding viral illness is the classic trigger for acute childhood post-viral ITP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Well, non-toxic, afebrile child (normal home-thermometer temperature)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A well, afebrile, non-toxic child lowers (but does not exclude) meningococcaemia; the safety-net for developing fever/toxicity remains.
Isolated bleeding in an otherwise well child fits ITP over an infiltrative/septic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of bone/joint pain, weight loss and night sweats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bone pain, weight loss and constitutional symptoms raise concern for marrow infiltration; their absence lowers but does not exclude leukaemia — only the film does.
Organomegaly suggests infiltrative malignancy; its absence lowers the probability. Sealed clinician finding — the AI cannot palpate.
Lymphadenopathy supports a lymphoproliferative process; its absence lowers the probability. Sealed clinician finding — not elicitable via telehealth.

loinc_ref.display: Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

investigation_details.expected_result: Isolated profound thrombocytopenia with normal white cells and haemoglobin
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of thrombocytopenia and the leukaemia exclusion remain unknown; management cannot be stratified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Isolated thrombocytopenia with an otherwise normal count is the defining laboratory feature of ITP. Requires bloods the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood film showing isolated thrombocytopenia with no blasts

investigation_details.expected_result: Reduced platelets, no circulating blasts, normal red and white cell morphology
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot exclude acute leukaemia — the critical discriminator from ITP.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The blood film (and marrow if indicated) is what distinguishes ITP from leukaemia; not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild tachycardia (HR ~110) with maintained perfusion and blood pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia may reflect blood loss or distress; maintained BP indicates current haemodynamic stability. Sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of headache, confusion, haematemesis or melaena

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Their absence is currently reassuring but does not remove the risk while the count is very low — these are the safety-net escalation triggers.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Isolated mucocutaneous bleeding in a well child
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Spontaneous mucosal bleeding plus petechiae/purpura in an otherwise well, afebrile child is the classic acute ITP constellation — but active bleeding makes it an emergency and the film is still required to exclude leukaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood examination with platelet count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the severity of thrombocytopenia and screens the other cell lines; the pivotal test — performed in ED, not via telehealth.
Excludes blasts (leukaemia) and confirms isolated thrombocytopenia.
Excludes DIC and a coagulopathic cause of bleeding.
Preparedness for transfusion given active bleeding and unknown count.
Not first-line in typical acute childhood ITP; reserved for atypical features or before certain therapies, and a specialist/hospital decision — never an AI telehealth action.
